### Powers and duties of litigation friend E+W


16.14. —(1) The litigation friend—


(2) Where the litigation friend is an officer of the Service or a Welsh family proceedings officer, rule 16.20 applies as it applies to a children's guardian appointed in accordance with Chapter 6.


### Procedure where appointment of litigation friend comes to an end E+W


16.15. —(1) When a child who is not a protected party reaches the age of 18, a litigation friend's appointment comes to an end.


(2) A court officer will send a notice to the other parties stating that the appointment of the child's litigation friend to act has ended.


### CHAPTER 6 E+W CHILDREN'S GUARDIAN APPOINTED UNDER RULE 16.3


### Application of this Chapter E+W


16.16. This Chapter applies where the court must appoint a children's guardian in accordance with rule 16.3.


### Who may be a children's guardian E+W


16.17. Where the court is appointing a children's guardian under rule 16.3 it will appoint an officer of the Service or a Welsh family proceedings officer.


### What the court or a court officer will do once the court has made a decision about appointing a children's guardian E+W


16.18. —(1) Where the court appoints a children's guardian under rule 16.3 a court officer will record the appointment and, as soon as practicable, will—


(2) A court officer has a continuing duty to send the children's guardian a copy of any other document filed with the court during the course of the proceedings.


### Termination of the appointment of the children's guardian E+W


16.19. —(1) The appointment of a children's guardian under rule 16.3 continues for such time as is specified in the appointment or until terminated by the court.


(2) When terminating an appointment in accordance with paragraph (1), the court will give reasons for doing so, a note of which will be taken by the court or a court officer.


### Powers and duties of the children's guardian E+W
